epigraph.quote: We have seen a heron, kingfisher, large fish, minnows, lizards, a deer, many coppiced sweet chestnuts (ground for flour).

per_stop.quote: We had lunch on the Shuttle. No water can be poured, or washing up or coffee.
The clear drop of the slender pillars from ceiling to floor along the nave is spectacular.
Between the canal & the lake were rows & rows of poplars. Very pretty restful, glinting sunlight, fish, frogs.
Good clean loos, free showers. Cement ping pong tables + nets.
Simon did an amazing Tour de Force driving across the roundabout at the L'Arc de Triomphe.
A man has made it his life's work to renovate it for the last 10 years.
The boys set off to the river in the 2 rubber dinghies + have a very good time.
There is a notice up of a tombola on Aug 9th. The first prize is a pig; the second, a lamb, the 3rd-6th is a ham (Dead or alive?)
We had coffee in the lovely square with its canopy of plane trees.
The cave is big enough to sit Notre Dame Paris inside.
We parked under a weeping willow. Simon again drove into the tree.
a beautiful old town, but rather over-restored - lots of gift shops.
We spent the night at Monastier which was mentioned by Stevenson in his Travels with a donkey.
